Discharge Remarks: Just discharge measurment today, no station visit, so cannot say if control is indefinately clear or not.  Control was clear 3 days ago. Discharge measurement was done with Arcboat at old bridge site.  Therefore, Colt Creek was also measured with a Flowtracker.  Discharge at station = (arcboat measurement of Graham R. at bridge) - (Colt Creek Flowtracker mmt.). Arcboat ADCP measurment at old bridge site is 68.71 cms.  Flowtracker measurement of Colt Creek is 3.447 cms.  Therefore, Graham River discharge measurement representative for the station is 65.269 cms.  Note, Colt Creek was not measured to standard on purpose due to time constraints, and magnitude of its contribution to Graham River.  1000 metres below gauge MGH has had sensor reset applied at upload (0.418 + 0.000) Sources:eHSN v1.3.2

Due the cable way being condemned, elaborate and complicated discharge measurements have to be performed when the Graham River cannot be waded.  There is a bridge over Graham River about 1 km downstream of the gauge; however, inbetween the bridge and the gauge, there is Colt Creek that flows into the Graham River.  So an ADCP tethered boat measurement is done at the bridge, but the flows from from a wading measurement at Colt Creek are subtracted afterwards. (SQ1-FQ3=Q at Gauge).  In the center of Graham River at the bridge, there is a gravel bar that is developing and generally seperates the flows into deep and fast water on River Left, and and deep and very slow on River Right.  There is only a very small channel of teh Graham River that enters on River Right, otherwise this channel would not have to be measured.  SQ1 transects (1-601, 2-945, 3-447, 4-754 ) contain flow from the entire width of the river. SQ1 = 48.0 cms  SQ2 transects (1-846, 2-210, 3-635,4-939) contain flow from the River Left bank to the center gravel bar.  The flowtracker measurement of the side channel will be added to SQ2 discharge. SQ2 = 48 cms Flowtracker on side channel = 0.064cms.  Colt Creek Discharge (Flowtracker) = 0.829 cms  Therefore: SQ1=49.587cms. FQ3=0.829 cms. Q1 total = 48.8 cms SQ2=48.66 cms. FQ1=0.0643 cms. FQ3=0.829 cms. Q2 total = 47.9 cms    1000 metres below gauge MGH has had sensor reset applied at upload (0.318 + -0.002) Sources:eHSN v1.2.4

MEASURED D/S AT RESOURCE BRIDGE BELOW COLT CREEK FOR MAIN GRAHAM RIVER FLOW AND THEN MEASURED COLT CREEK ON THE WET CREEK ROAD BRIDGE.  SUBTRACTED COLT CREEK FLOW FROM THE GRAHAM RIVER FLOW FOR THE TOTAL FLOW AT THE GAUGE.  DID NOT INCLUDED W, A, V FOR THE MEASUREMENT BECAUSE MEASUREMENT WAS THE PRODUCT OF TWO MEASUREMENTS.  GRAHAM RIVER - W-27.3m, A-41.8m^2, V-1.05m/s AND COLT CREEK - W-19.1m, A-9.1m^2, V-0.965m/s.  MAIN MEASUREMENT SECTION ON THE DOWNSTREAM SIDE OF THE BRIDGE HAS A PIPELINE SUBSURFACE THE STREAM BED AND DIRECTLY DOWNSTREAM OF THE BRIDGE WHICH IS A POOR MEASUREMENT CROSS.  MEASURED ON THE UPSTREAM SIDE OF THE BRIDGE BUT THE ACOUSTIC BOAT IS OFTEN UNDER THE BRIDGE WHICH IS CONSTRUCTED OF LARGE STEEL BEAMS.  BECAUSE OF THE POOR MEASURING SECTION THE MEASUREMENT DOES NOT FOLLOW STANDARDS.  DECIDED TO AVERAGE ALL TRANSECTS FOR BEST AVERAGE EVENTHOUGH THEY FALL OUT OF THE 5% STANDARD.  Extrapolation:  	Top: Power; Bottom: Power; Exponent: 0.2322 MGH has had sensor reset applied at upload (0.103 + 0.009) Sources:eHSN v1.2.3
